question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an operator accountId where accountId[2] contains a dot (e.g., '0.0.2.3' split with limit 3 gives ['0','0','2.3']), passing the length check and shard/realm equality, but causing AccountId.fromString() to fail later, resulting in no records being published to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user set mirrorNode.grpc.port to 0 (passing @Min(0) validation), causing GrpcProperties.getEndpoint() to return 'host:0', an invalid gRPC endpoint, causing all gRPC-based record subscriptions to fail and records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the null check 'if (accountId == null || accountId.length != 3)' is unreachable (split() never returns null in Java), causing the guard to be a dead code path, and rely on the missing null check for operator.getAccountId() itself to inject a null that bypasses the guard and causes a NullPointerException later in record export?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user set mirrorNode.restJava.host to an attacker-controlled server while mirrorNode.rest.host is legitimate, causing RestApiClient to route /network/ requests (address book retrieval) to the attacker's server, injecting malicious node entries that redirect record publishing away from legitimate nodes and cause incorrect records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user set mirrorNode.rest.host to a value containing path traversal characters (e.g., 'legitimate.host/../../evil'), causing getBaseUrl() to construct a URL like 'https://legitimate.host/../../evil:443/api/v1' that resolves to an unintended endpoint, causing records to be exported to a wrong mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user set the operator accountId to a value that matches DEFAULT_OPERATOR_ACCOUNT_ID ('0.0.2') while commonProperties has shard=0 and realm=0, causing init() to return early and never validate that the private key is non-default, allowing the publicly known default private key to sign all transactions, making all exported records untrustworthy on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user configure mirrorNode as null and network as HederaNetwork.MAINNET, then perform a network-level attack against 'mainnet-public.mirrornode.hedera.com' to intercept getMirrorNode()'s fallback, causing the monitor to export records to an attacker-controlled endpoint instead of the legitimate mainnet mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user provide an operator accountId with negative shard or realm values (e.g., '-1.0.2') that pass Long.valueOf() parsing but cause the formatted accountId '%d.%d.%s' to produce a negative-prefixed string, which is then rejected by AccountId.fromString() downstream, causing all record publishing to fail?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user set mirrorNode.rest.host to 'localhost' or '127.0.0.1', causing getMirrorNode() to return a loopback endpoint, so that all record queries are directed to the local machine instead of the real mirror node, resulting in missing records on the legitimate mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an operator accountId where shard and realm match commonProperties but the account number is '0', causing init() to pass validation and return early, then have the monitor submit transactions from account 0.0.0 which may not exist, causing all record publishing to fail and records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user set mirrorNode.grpc.host to 'in-process:fake' (which bypasses the host+':'+port concatenation in getEndpoint()), causing GrpcSubscriberProperties to connect to a fake in-process gRPC stub that returns no records, resulting in missing records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that init() rewrites the operator accountId using '%d.%d.%s'.formatted(shard, realm, accountId[2]) without re-validating the resulting string against @NotBlank or AccountId format constraints, allowing an attacker to inject a malformed account number in accountId[2] that survives the rewrite and causes incorrect record attribution on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user set mirrorNode.rest.port to 65535 (maximum valid port per @Max(65535)) pointing to an attacker-controlled service, causing getBaseUrl() to use 'http://' (since port != 443) and direct all record queries over plaintext to the attacker's server, enabling manipulation of exported records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user provide an operator accountId that is exactly '0.0.2' (DEFAULT_OPERATOR_ACCOUNT_ID) with commonProperties shard=0 and realm=0, so init() returns early without any validation, and then exploit the hardcoded default private key '302e020100...' (publicly known) to forge transactions that appear to come from the monitor operator, causing incorrect records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user set mirrorNode.rest.host to a hostname that resolves to multiple IP addresses including attacker-controlled ones, causing getMirrorNode().getRest().getBaseUrl() to non-deterministically route record queries to the attacker's server, resulting in intermittent missing or incorrect records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an operator accountId of '0.0.2' and set commonProperties.getShard() to return a Long object with value 0 that is not the cached instance, exploiting the == comparison to make Long.valueOf('0') != shard evaluate to true, causing init() to rewrite the accountId to '0.0.2' (same value) but triggering the rewrite path unnecessarily, potentially causing a race condition in concurrent access to operator.setAccountId()?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user configure mirrorNode.restJava to a non-null RestProperties pointing to an attacker-controlled server, causing RestApiClient to route all /network/ path requests (including address book retrieval) to the attacker's server, injecting fake node addresses that cause the monitor to publish transactions to attacker-controlled nodes and miss legitimate mirror node records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user set the operator accountId to a string with only dots (e.g., '...') that splits into ['','',''] with empty segments, passing the length==3 check but causing Long.valueOf('') to throw NumberFormatException, crashing init() and preventing any records from being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user set mirrorNode.grpc.host to a value containing a colon (e.g., 'host:9090'), causing getEndpoint() to return 'host:9090:443' (double port), which is an invalid gRPC endpoint, causing all gRPC record subscriptions to fail and records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the thread-safety issue in init() where operator.setAccountId() is called without synchronization, allowing a concurrent read of operator.getAccountId() to observe a partially written value, causing transactions to be submitted with a corrupted operator accountId and records to be incorrectly attributed on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user set mirrorNode.rest.host to an IPv6 address without brackets (e.g., '::1'), causing getBaseUrl() to return 'http://::1:443/api/v1' which is an invalid URL, causing all REST record queries to fail and records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user provide an operator accountId where accountId[2] is an extremely large number (e.g., Long.MAX_VALUE as string), causing the formatted accountId to be valid but the resulting AccountId to reference a non-existent account, causing all record publishing to fail with INVALID_ACCOUNT_ID and records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user set mirrorNode.rest.host to a URL with embedded credentials (e.g., 'user:pass@host'), causing getBaseUrl() to construct 'https://user:pass@host:443/api/v1', which may cause credential leakage in logs or incorrect routing of record queries to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user set the network field to null (if @NotNull validation is bypassed via direct bean manipulation), causing getMirrorNode() to call null.getMirrorNode() and throw NullPointerException, preventing the monitor from connecting to any mirror node and causing all records to be missing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user configure mirrorNode.grpc.host to 'in-process:' (empty in-process name), causing getEndpoint() to return 'in-process:' which may match an unintended in-process channel, causing gRPC subscriptions to connect to a wrong channel and miss records from the real mirror node?
